questions.question: Akbar was only _____ years old when he was proclaimed emperor.
questions.explanation: Akbar ascended to the throne at the young age of 13.

questions.question: Akbar ruled the Mughal Empire for _____ years, from 1556 to 1605 CE.
questions.explanation: Akbar's reign lasted 50 years, making it one of the longest and most significant in Mughal history.

questions.question: _____ served as Akbar's regent during his early years as emperor.
questions.explanation: Bairam Khan was appointed as the regent to guide the young emperor until he was old enough to rule independently.

questions.question: Akbar dismissed Bairam Khan at the age of _____.
questions.explanation: At 18, Akbar took personal control of the administration by dismissing his regent Bairam Khan.

questions.question: In the Second Battle of Panipat (1556), Akbar's forces defeated _____.
questions.explanation: Hemu was the Hindu general who challenged Mughal authority and was defeated at the Second Battle of Panipat.

questions.question: Akbar annexed Gondwana in the year _____.
questions.explanation: Gondwana was annexed in 1564, after Malwa (1561) and before Gujarat (1572).

questions.question: Bengal was conquered by Akbar during the period _____.
questions.explanation: The conquest of Bengal took place between 1574 and 1576.

questions.question: Akbar married the daughter of the Raja of _____ as part of his Rajput policy.
questions.explanation: Akbar's marriage alliance with the Rajput princess of Jaipur (Amber) was a key part of his policy of reconciliation with Rajput rulers.

questions.question: The Rajput commander at the siege of Chittor was _____.
questions.explanation: Jai Mal led the defence of Chittor against Akbar's forces.

questions.question: During the fall of Chittor, the Rajput women performed _____ to preserve their honour.
questions.explanation: Jauhar was the practice of self-immolation by Rajput women to avoid capture by the enemy.

questions.question: The Battle of Haldighati was fought in the year _____.
questions.explanation: The famous Battle of Haldighati took place in 1576 between Rana Pratap and Akbar's forces led by Raja Man Singh.

questions.question: Akbar's empire was divided into _____ subas (provinces).
questions.explanation: For administrative efficiency, Akbar divided his empire into 15 subas or provinces.

questions.question: The subas were subdivided into sarkars, which were further divided into _____.
questions.explanation: The three-tier administrative structure was: subas (provinces), sarkars (districts), and parganas (sub-districts).

questions.question: In the Mansabdari system, ranks ranged from 10 to _____ horsemen.
questions.explanation: The Mansabdari system had ranks ranging from 10 to 7000 horsemen, and these ranks were not hereditary.

questions.question: The _____ was responsible for military affairs in Akbar's council of ministers.
questions.explanation: The Mir Bakshi handled all military administration and maintained records of the army.

questions.question: The minister in charge of the royal household was called the _____.
questions.explanation: The Mir Saman looked after the royal household and its requirements.

questions.question: Todar Mal introduced the bamboo jarib with _____ for accurate land measurement.
questions.explanation: Iron rings were attached to the bamboo jarib to prevent it from shrinking or expanding, ensuring accurate land measurement.

questions.question: Under Todar Mal's Bandobast, the land revenue was fixed at _____ of the average produce.
questions.explanation: Todar Mal fixed the land revenue at one-third of the average produce, which was considered a fair rate.

questions.question: Akbar abolished the _____ tax and the pilgrim tax on Hindus.
questions.explanation: To promote religious harmony, Akbar abolished the jizya (a tax on non-Muslims) and the pilgrim tax.

questions.question: The Ibadat Khana was built by Akbar at _____ in 1575.
questions.explanation: The Ibadat Khana (House of Worship) was constructed at Fatehpur Sikri for religious discussions among scholars of different faiths.

questions.question: Akbar founded a new faith called _____ in 1582.
questions.explanation: Din-i-Ilahi (Divine Faith) was Akbar's attempt to create a syncretic religion combining elements of various faiths.

questions.question: Akbar's principle of universal peace was known as _____.
questions.explanation: Sulh-i-Kul (universal peace) was Akbar's guiding principle of tolerance towards all religions.

questions.question: _____ was the famous Persian poet in Akbar's court, while _____ wrote the Akbarnama.
questions.explanation: Faizi was Akbar's court poet who composed in Persian, and his brother Abul Fazl was the historian who authored the Akbarnama.

questions.question: Akbar's library contained _____ books, even though he himself was illiterate.
questions.explanation: Despite being illiterate, Akbar was a great patron of learning and maintained a library of 24,000 books.

questions.question: The _____ at Fatehpur Sikri is a famous gateway built by Akbar, and the city was primarily constructed in _____.
questions.explanation: The Buland Darwaza (Gate of Magnificence) is one of the highest gateways in the world. Fatehpur Sikri was built primarily of red sandstone.
